mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment remains below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both show positive monthly changes
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase indicates supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing output that immediately depresses equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes during late-cycle phases produce positive 5-day returns in over 50% of cases, or the predictive coefficient flips sign when tested against early-cycle data.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL increase exceeding 0.4% signals accelerating inflation that forces markets to price in tighter future monetary policy, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 yields positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of high-inflation months, or the signal's Rank IC drops below zero during periods of stable inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and rising household income, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS revisions fail to predict positive 60-day returns in at least 55% of monthly observations, or the correlation coefficient remains below 0.05 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth decelerates while real personal consumption expenditures accelerate indicates unsustainable consumer leverage that precedes a demand shock and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 2.0% and RSAFS monthly growth > 1.5%
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence pattern produces positive 20-day returns in over 50% of historical instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: An expansion in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals rapid capacity utilization and margin improvement that immediately lifts equity prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes during recovery phases yield negative 5-day returns in more than 45% of cases, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in out-of-sample recovery periods.
